title: New checkpoint flake
body:
We have a new checkpoint flake, and it's hitting us hard this week. Unfortunately it's one of those ginkgo tests that offer zero visibility into the problem, and I don't have the time or motivation today (this is my day off) to come up with a reproducer and full log. So here you go.

### Podman checkpoint [It] podman checkpoint a container with volumes 
* fedora-35 : int remote fedora-35 root host [remote]
  * PR #14637
    * [06-17 09:08](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5181533702062080/html/int-remote-fedora-35-root-host.log.html#t--podman-checkpoint-a-container-with-volumes--1)
    * [06-17 09:08](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5181533702062080/html/int-remote-fedora-35-root-host.log.html#t--podman-checkpoint-a-container-with-volumes--2)

I've added the `remote` label because, so far, all failures are f35/f36 remote.

This is an incomplete list: it does not include currently-open PRs.

@containers/podman-maintainers please treat this as high priority.

Well, AFAICT this is just a simple trivial race condition, a difference between podman and podman-remote.
* **podman** guarantees that, after `checkpoint` runs, the container is 100% gone
* **podman-remote** does not. There is a brief window in which the container is `Exited`.

(This may be a bug in `run --rm`, not `checkpoint`: historically there have been a number of races in `podman-remote run --rm`).

Reproducer: save this as `/tmp/doit`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

REMOTE=
if [[ -n "$1" ]]; then
    REMOTE=remote
fi

pm() {
    if [[ -n "$REMOTE" ]]; then
        podman-remote "$@"
    else
        podman "$@"
    fi
}

while :;do
    cid=$(pm run -d --rm quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 top)
    rm -f /tmp/foo.tar.gz
    pm container checkpoint $cid -e /tmp/foo.tar.gz
    psa=$(pm ps -a --noheading)
    if [[ -n "$psa" ]]; then
            echo "************** FAILED  $psa"
            exit 1
    fi
done
```
Fire up a server (`podman system service -t 0 &`), then run `/tmp/doit remote`. It will fail pretty quickly. If you run it without `remote`, it seems to run forever.

Side note: `podman container checkpoint` spits out a CID. `podman-remote` spits out an empty line. Seems like a bug to me. If someone fixes this issue, could you piggyback that fix into your PR?
